statement: AMD must split its scarce ~11% CoWoS allocation between Venice EPYC CPUs and MI400 Instinct GPUs, so each EPYC packaging slot used cannot be allocated to an Instinct GPU.
why: Internal cannibalization of a fixed, reserved CoWoS allocation caps how many MI400 Instinct GPUs AMD can ship, a supply bottleneck AMD cannot relieve on short notice.

impact.mechanism: Shared packaging capacity between AMD's CPU and GPU lines constrains MI400 accelerator supply because allocation is reserved years in advance.

evidence.excerpt: Each EPYC slot utilized cannot be allocated to an Instinct GPU

confidence.basis: Single secondary source (Forbes); no primary filing.
